description: Retractable chrome claws that interface with RoombaCore systems.
effect: +3 to hacking actions, 1d6 bonus damage vs drones

description: Military grade. Creates a dot so compelling enemies must chase it for one turn.
effect: Stun enemy for 1 turn, 1 charge per combat

description: Pre-corporate catnip of legendary quality. Effects are unpredictable but always dramatic.
effect: Roll 1d6: 1-2 restore 50hp, 3-4 deal 50 damage to random enemy, 5-6 both

description: Restore 40hp. The mystery is why it tastes this good.
A sleek jacket with embedded LED strips. Reduces laser damage and looks incredible.
Salvaged from a defeated RoombaCore drone. Can be sold or used to craft.
effect: Sell for 15 CreditChips or combine 3 for HackingClaws

description: A master key card granting access to all RoombaCore systems. The resistance has wanted this for years.
effect: Unlocks RoombaCoreTower secured areas, +5 to all hacking actions

description: The processing core of an Elite RoombaCore drone. Highly valuable on the black market.
effect: Sell for 50 CreditChips or upgrade HackingClaws

description: The dark essence of the Vacuum Demon, crystallized into a dense black sphere. Hums ominously.
effect: Legendary item: grants immunity to vacuum damage type

description: A large molar from a mutant sewer rat. Good luck charm, apparently.
Armor forged from the scales of the legendary NeoTokyo Dragon Cat. Mythic rarity.
effect: -5 to all damage received, +2 to all stats

description: The CEO Roomba's business suit, sized for a disc-shaped robot. Stylish in a bleak way.
effect: Trophy item. Equip for +10 gold per combat as enemies respect the suit.

description: Pre-corporate currency. Most merchants won't take it but a few nostalgic types will.
effect: Worth 30 CreditChips to the right merchant

description: Neo-Pawsburg's standard digital currency. RoombaCore issues them. The resistance uses them anyway.
